entry.title: Vendor blocked for posting
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I'm new to SAP and I have been working for a few weeks now on MDG tasks.

I have one vendor which is blocked from posting and I need to know how this block was triggered. In the change history I get SAP_WFRT but this is general for any system change, and it's not helping. The change could have came from non-SAP system but how can I trace this back?

Appreciate all the help.

entry.title: How to Use Multiple APIs in a Single iFlow (SAP Integration Suite)
entry.content:
In real-time integration scenarios, we commonly use multiple APIs within a single integration flow (iFlow). For example, one API response is required as input for another API, or the final response may be the combination of the both API responses.

SAP Integration Suite (CPI) provides components like content modifier, adapters that helps us for multiple APIs scenarios in one iFlow.

In this blog, we will walk through a step-by-step implementation of calling two APIs within a single iFlow, capturing each response, and finally combining or processing the data before sending it back to the final response. 

Overview 

  • user triggers the iFlow using an HTTPS sender 
  • The iFlow calls API 1, captures its response
  • With in response of the API 1 it calls API 2, captures its response 
  • Finally, it processes and returns the combined responses of both APIs 

Step-by-Step Implementation 

Step 1: Create an iFlow 

Create a new Integration Flow (iFlow) in SAP Integration Suite as shown in the below figure. 

HTTPS sender --> iFlow logic (it will sequentially call the two APIs) --> HTTP receiver

 

Step 2: Configure the HTTPS Sender Adapter 

  • In the sender side give the address in the connection tab(endpoint path). It is basically the endpoint used to trigger externally (for example in our case it's postman).
  • In our iFlow I have given the address as /testweatherNote: Make sure the address starts with /<address>

 

Step 3: Configure Content Modifier 1 (API 1 Headers and Properties) 

Message Header 

Use content modifier header to externally call the API using it's host and key values.

Add the host and key value of the first API.

 Exchange Properties 

Use exchange properties to fetch the input values dynamically.

  • Name: Input tag name 
  • Source Type: XPath / Expression 
  • Source Value: XML input tag 

This property will later be used in the API call.

 Step 4: Configure Receiver 1 (HTTP Adapter – API 1) 

Based on the input, response is fetched from the external API via request reply and HTTP receiver adapter.

This request reply fetches the response from the external API and send to the next step of the flow.

  • In the Receiver 1 choose HTTP Adapter.
  • Go to Connections and Provide
    Address:  Address will be the API 1 endpoint 
    Query parameters: In query parameter to fetch the response with dynamic input use ${property.<propertyname>} expression(This allows us to dynamically read the value) .

This step performs the actual call to the first API. 

 Step 5: Configure Content Modifier 2 (Store API 1 Response) 

Add 2nd Content Modifier to store the response of the 1st API.

In the exchange properties add 

  • Name: response1 
  • Source Type: Expression 
  • Source Value: ${in.body} 

This stores the first API response for later use. 

 Step 6: Configure Content Modifier 3 (API 2 Headers)

Now let us call the 2nd API. As done in the earlier step we'll use the content modifier to call the 2nd API

Add the host and key value of the second API.

These headers may differ from API 1 depending on the provider. 

In Exchange property add the properties required to fetch the response from API 2.

Step 7: Configure Receiver 2 (HTTP Adapter – API 2) 

Add request reply and HTTP receiver adapter to fetch the response from the 2nd API.

  • In the Receiver 2 choose HTTP Adapter.
  • Go to Connections and Provide
    Address:  Address will be the API 2 endpoint 
    Query parameters: In query parameter to fetch the response with dynamic input use ${property.<propertyname>} expression(This allows us to dynamically read the value) .

This step triggers the second API call.

Step 8: Configure Content Modifier 4 (Store API 2 Response)  

Add 4th Content Modifier to store the response of the 2nd API.

In the exchange properties add 

  • Name: response2 
  • Source Type: Expression 
  • Source Value: ${in.body}

This stores the response of the second API. 

Step 9: Configure Content Modifier 5 (Final Message Body) 

Add content modifier 5 to store the responses of Both the APIs. This will give us the combined output of both APIs

In Content Modifier 5 Message Body Set Type: Expression and in body add

  • ${property.response1} 
  • ${property.response2} 

This step prepares the final response that combines or processes both API responses.

Step 10: Save and Deploy the iFlow 

  • Save the iFlow 
  • Deploy it successfully 

Step 11: Test Using Postman 

  • Copy the generated HTTPS endpoint 
  • Paste it into Postman 
  • Send the request 
  • Verify the combined output from both APIs  

 

Conclusion/Summary

In this blog we have covered how can we use multiple APIs in a single iFlow with help of content modifier. Also learnt about how to call external API using content modifier headers and reading the dynamic input from the postman using the content modifier exchange properties. We also saw how to take API 1 response as input for API 2 and in final result using content modifier message body we combined the both APIs responses. By this approach developers can design scalable and reusable iFlows in the real time scenarios.

entry.title: Annual inventory report/compensation for similar products
entry.content:
Dear all, 

I would like to know whether there is a current SAP S/4Hana functionality that allows the netting/compensation for similar goods, for the annual inventory report.

Thank you,

entry.title: Building a Real‑Time Currency Conversion iFlow using SAP Cloud Integration (CPI)
entry.content:
Introduction

While learning SAP Cloud Integration (CPI), I wanted to work on a practical, real‑time use case rather than a basic file‑based or sample integration. In many real projects, applications need to handle multiple currencies because of global customers, suppliers, and financial transactions.

To understand how CPI handles API‑based synchronous integrations, I decided to build a currency conversion iFlow. The objective was to expose an HTTPS endpoint, call an external currency conversion API, and return the converted amount in a readable format. Along with the happy path, I also wanted to implement proper exception handling, similar to what is expected in real production scenarios.

This blog shares my design approach, iFlow structure, and key learnings from implementing this scenario.

Business Requirement

The requirements I considered while designing this iFlow were:

  • Convert an amount from one currency to another in real time
  • Expose the functionality through a secure HTTPS endpoint
  • Fetch up‑to‑date exchange rates from an external API
  • Return the result in a user‑friendly output
  • Handle errors in a centralized and structured way

This closely mimics real‑world requirements where CPI acts as an integration layer between business applications and external services.

High‑Level Solution Overview

I designed the solution using SAP Cloud Integration following a simple API‑to‑API pattern:

  1. An external system sends a currency conversion request.
  2. CPI receives and processes the request.
  3. CPI calls an external currency conversion API.
  4. CPI formats the response as an HTML output.
  5. In case of any failure, CPI handles errors through an Exception Subprocess and sends notifications.

This approach helped me understand how CPI manages synchronous calls, properties, transformations, and error handling together.

iFlow Design Overview

The iFlow is divided into two main parts:

  • Main Integration Flow – handles the successful processing
  • Global Exception Subprocess – handles errors from any step in the flow

This separation made the design clean and easier to maintain.

I-FLow.

Main Integration Flow – Step‑by‑Step

HTTPS_In

I used an HTTPS sender adapter as the entry point of the iFlow. This allows external systems or tools like Postman to send requests in JSON format.

This step starts the message processing and generates the Message Processing Log (MPL) ID, which is useful for monitoring and troubleshooting.

Why I used it:
Most real‑time integrations in CPI are API‑driven, so HTTPS was the natural choice.

HTTPS sender adapter configuration.

You can choose your own address name. 

JSON2XML_Convert

Since the incoming request is in JSON format, I added a JSON to XML converter. This made it easier to work with the payload in CPI, especially for extracting values using content modifiers or XPath.

Learning:
Converting early helps avoid complications in later steps.

Set_ConvParams (Content Modifier)

In this step, I extracted the source currency, target currency, and amount and stored them as Exchange Properties.

I purposely used properties instead of reading values repeatedly from the payload, as this improves readability and reusability across the flow.

Why I did this:
Using properties keeps later steps independent of payload structure.

Content Modifier (Properties tab)

Call_RateAPI

Here, I used a Request‑Reply pattern to call an external currency conversion API. Since the requirement was to return the converted amount immediately, a synchronous call was necessary.

During testing, I intentionally triggered API errors to verify whether my exception handling was working correctly.

Learning:
Request‑Reply is ideal when immediate responses are required.

 

Build_HTML_Result

After receiving the API response, I used a Groovy script to extract the converted amount and build an HTML‑formatted output.

Instead of returning raw JSON or plain text, I formatted the result as an HTML table containing:

  • Source currency
  • Target currency
  • Source amount
  • Converted amount
  • Timestamp (IST)

Why I did this:
HTML output improves readability for users, testers, and demos.

HTTP_Out

Finally, the formatted HTML response is sent back to the caller. This allows the result to be previewed directly in Postman or a browser.

Use case:
This is very helpful during testing and demonstrations without needing a separate UI.

Exception Subprocess – Centralized Error Handling

While building the flow, I realized that handling only the success scenario was not enough. So I implemented a Global Exception Subprocess to handle failures consistently.

Err_Start

This step is triggered automatically whenever an error occurs in any part of the main flow, such as:

  • API failures
  • Groovy errors
  • Payload or conversion issues

Err_Build_HTML

In this step, I extracted error details such as:

  • MPL ID
  • Error type
  • Error message
  • Timestamp

I formatted these details into an HTML table, similar to the success response. This made the error output structured and easy to understand.

Learning:
Readable error messages save a lot of troubleshooting time.

Mail_Out_Err

I added a Mail adapter to send the formatted error details to a support email ID. This ensures that failures are noticed immediately without checking CPI logs manually.

Real‑world relevance:
This is commonly used in production environments for monitoring.

Err_End

The exception flow ends cleanly after sending the notification, ensuring proper message lifecycle completion.

The above one is for incorrect input payload 

Benefits of This Approach

  • Practical understanding of API‑based integrations in SAP CPI
  • Importance of Exchange Properties for clean design
  • How to implement a reusable Exception Subprocess
  • Benefits of HTML‑formatted responses for both success and errors
  • How CPL logs (MPL ID) help in monitoring and support

Conclusion

This exercise helped me gain hands‑on experience in building a real‑time integration scenario using SAP Cloud Integration. By implementing both success and error handling properly, I was able to design an iFlow that closely resembles a real‑world project.

The same design pattern can be reused for many API‑driven integration scenarios, making it a valuable learning experience.

entry.title: Syntax errors after manual implementation of DRC France SAP Notes
entry.content:
After manually implementing all SAP Notes related to DRC France, and after checking the configuration points, we are facing multiple dumps (SYNTAX_ERROR) in the EDO framework.

1. Configuration point: EDOGENCUSTV

  • Program: SAPLEDOT4
  • Associated Note: 3518185
  • Error:
    The field "EDOORGUNITV_EXTRACT" is unknown, but there are similar fields:
    "EDOGENCUSTV_EXTRACT", "EDOFUNCTIONV_EXTRACT", "EDOACTIONUIMETHV_EXTRACT"

2. Configuration point: EDOT158VV

  • Program: SAPLEDOT6
  • Associated Note: 3647347
  • Error:
    INCLUDE report "LEDOT6T00" not found

3. Configuration points: EDOEUBUPAV, EDOEUCOMPV, EDOEUBPIDTYPEV, EDOEUBANKACCV

  • Program: SAPLEDOT_EU
  • Associated Note: 3518281
  • Error:
    The field "EDOEUMAPCLSALGV_EXTRACT" is unknown, but there are similar fields:
    "EDOEUCOMPV_EXTRACT", "EDOEUCUSTV_EXTRACT", "EDOEUBUPAV_EXTRACT"

Context:

  • Manual implementation of SAP Notes (no TCI)
  • Errors occur during configuration

Request:

Could you please confirm:

  • If some prerequisite notes are missing or inconsistently implemented
  • Or if a specific correction is required

entry.title: Custom rule so that WT /127 and WT /413 brought forward till the last period
entry.content:
Dear Team,

We have processed payments through Infotype 0015 for January 2026 only and currently use two PCRs as outlined below:

* PCR 1 (placed at INN1):

ADDWT *
ADDWT /127

* PCR 2 (placed at INE0):

ADDWTE*

The /127 value is being generated and reflected in Gross Salary.

The payment is not being made in the subsequent months. As a result, neither the /127 value nor the wage type value is consistently updated in Gross Salary.

We would appreciate guidance on creating the correct custom rule in line with SAP Note 3036551 to resolve this issue.

Thank you for your support.

Best regards

entry.title: Re: What should an SD Consultant without experience learn...
entry.content:
Hi @Tatiana_Stingl 
Congratutation. 
I recommend to share your badge and promote your achievements! 
You can share your badge on social media like LinkedIn, Twitter or Facebook. The badge icon can also be added to your email signature. You can also embed it into your own web page or save it locally as a PDF print version. ​

SAP automatically shows your badges in your Learner Profile in SAP Learning Hub and in the One Public Profile on sap.com in case you enable the feature to make your badges visible to others. By allowing this, other users will have a better overview about who you are when they interact with you, e.g. in blogs or discussions on SAP's help portal. ​

Get the maximum transparency by joining the directory(opens in new tab) feature in order to be found by others based on your skills, badges or other search options. 

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: QM Notification - "An inspection lot on sample plant already exists"
entry.content:
Hello Team

While Creating QM Notification we are facing this Error "An inspection lot on sample plant already exists".

The issue from Both Environment Quality and Production for some material. Currently I am doing testing in quality. The attachment also from quality.

I request to please check and share some solution to clear this error.

Thanks

entry.title: Re: Introducing UI5 Plugins for Claude: AI-Powered Development for SAPUI5 and OpenUI5

Installing plugin "ui5@claude-plugins-official"...
✘ Failed to install plugin "ui5@claude-plugins-official": Plugin "ui5" not found in marketplace "claude-plugins-official"

entry.title: SAP Build Process Automation call form in Browser after Trigger
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

At the beginning of the process, I launch a trigger form via a provided link after deployment to input the path to an invoice. This then starts an automation that extracts the relevant data from the invoice. The extracted data is subsequently passed to an approval form, which is currently displayed in the inbox.

Instead of opening the approval form in the inbox, I would prefer to remain in the browser and be automatically redirected to the form that displays the extracted data from the automation.
Is this possible?

Best Regards,

Carlo

entry.title: Re: Intelligent Mobile Expense Scanning
entry.content:
Dear All,

There does not appear to be a scoping question associated with the option “Do you want to enable data extraction from attachments for automatic expense creation?”. Could someone please provide guidance on what specifically needs to be scoped in this context?

@MarineOgier, have you successfully enabled this functionality?

entry.title: Re: Introducing UI5 Plugins for Claude: AI-Powered Development for SAPUI5 and OpenUI5
entry.content: Works on my machine. Please install the latest version of Claude Code. If it does not solve the issue, please create an issue https://github.com/UI5/plugins-claude/issues/new?template=1-bug-report.yml

entry.title: Re: CLM - Contract and Lease Management Migration in Publ...
entry.content:
Providing an update here so that it may assist others facing similar migration issue.

 
As per your suggestion @Chris1973  we rechecked everything, particularly the remaining lease payments. 
 
The migrated CLM “contract value” conditions representing the remaining future lease payments were correct however, the opening NPV (and ROU asset) values were overstated due to a manual correction made in the legacy S4 RE-FX contract that caused this inconsistency.
By removing our NPV statistical condition for this contract and allowing CLM to calculate the NPV from migrated cash flows + rate, we were able to see there was a difference between the valuation cashflows in S/4 legacy report and what CLM was calculating for NPV.  This led us to find a manual correction that was done in the S/4 contract that had over inflated the NPV and ROU asset values, leaving a liability balance remaining at the end of the contract term, evident in the valuation cashflow report.
 
As the data had already been migrated via the cockpit, the corrections have been made to the contract master data manually in Production, prior to now activating.  Issue resolved.
 
Thanks for your help
Sonya

entry.title: Re: Activate Joule in SAP Concur - a Step-by-Step Guide
entry.content:
Hi @nageshcaparthy 

Just to confirm that the Global IDs have been all provisioned succesfully to Concur. In S/4HANA priv. ed we can check some tables or display in SU01 to confirm the correct provisioning. Do we have something in Concur to validate?

entry.title: Restrict all users from a tcode
entry.content:
Hi,

I would like to restrict all the users which are having the Tcode - DHCDCMON but I want to keep 1 user out of restriction.
Can anyone suggest me what steps I can follow as an SAP Security person?

Thank you!

entry.title: Re: Invoice ERROR
entry.content:
The error occurs because certain conditions required for processing the invoice via EDI/SOA are not being met. Specifically, the system can only settle invoice line items received multiple times if:

  • The purchase order line item is set up for goods receipt–based invoice verification.
  • The goods receipt number (delivery note number) is included in the incoming IDoc or SOA message.
  • A differential invoice is used, with a previous invoice already existing (so the system can apply the two‑line item concept).

If any of these conditions are missing, the system cancels posting of the IDoc/SOA message.

Next Steps:

  1. Please check the IDoc or SOA message details to confirm whether the goods receipt number and purchase order line item are correct.
  2. If the supplier sent incorrect data (e.g., wrong PO line item), the message may be editable.
  3. If editing is not possible, kindly contact the supplier and request that they resend the invoice with corrected information.
  4. This will ensure the invoice can be processed successfully.

entry.title: Migrating CRM Sales Orders & Service Contracts to SAP S/4HANA CE -Single Order Framework Feasibility
entry.content:
We are migrating from SAP CRM to S/4HANA Customer Engagement. We have three specific questions:

  • For CRM Sales Orders — is migration to S/4HANA SD (VA01) the only standard path, or is there any CE equivalent?
  • For Service Contracts originating in SD — should these migrate to CE Service Contracts (Fiori App F2358) or remain in SD?
  • In CRM we used a Single Order Framework covering both Sales and Service in one transaction. What is the recommended end-to-end process design in S/4HANA CE, where Sales is handled
  • outside of CE? How do others handle document flow continuity across SD and CE?

 

We need additional investigation on this topic. Any input from community members or SAP product experts on standard approaches, BTP integration patterns, or known gaps would be greatly appreciated.

entry.title: Inquiry on SCCR Key Availability Without Active SAP Maintenance Support
entry.content:
Hello SAP ,

I have a question regarding SAP maintenance and SCCR keys.

If the SAP maintenance support is no longer active, can customers still request SCCR keys (e.g., license key, object key, hardware key, developer key)?

Looking forward to your advice and experience on this topic.

Thank you!

entry.title: Re: Key Figure Editability Horizon in SAP IBP
entry.content:
Hi @renegola92 ,

the editable and non-editable periods for the same Product A are overlapping. This means that the requirements contradict themselves. So if you want to make the periods 12.2025->infinity editable and in the same context 10.2025->infinity (i.e. FY 26), only the union of both works, i.e. 10.2025->infinity. Imho there is no standard way to tell the system for which period level a specific editability horizon shall be applied in case the underlying key figures share the same base planning level.

BR, Peter

entry.title: Building Custom Joule Skills via RFC: Guide for SAP S/4HANA and ECC Integration
entry.content:
Introduction

In today’s digital enterprise landscape, rapid access to critical business data can significantly impact operational efficiency and customer satisfaction. SAP’s AI-powered digital assistant, Joule, offers organizations the ability to interact with their enterprise systems through natural language conversations. By extending Joule with custom skills, businesses can create tailored experiences that bring real-time data directly to their teams—without the need for complex navigation through traditional ERP interfaces.

This article provides a comprehensive technical guide for building custom Joule skills that connect to SAP S/4HANA or ECC systems via RFC (Remote Function Call). We’ll explore a practical use case and walk through the complete configuration process.

Business Scenario

Consider a common challenge faced by sales and operations teams: A customer inquires about the manufacturing progress of their order. Traditionally, the specialist would need to log into the ERP system, navigate through multiple screens, and manually locate the production order details. This process is time-consuming and interrupts the flow of customer communication.

With a custom Joule skill, this same specialist can simply ask Joule in natural language—"What’s the status of production order 12345?"—and receive immediate, formatted information directly within SAP Build Workzone. This streamlined approach enhances productivity and improves response times to customer inquiries.

 

 

Solution Architecture

The technical architecture comprises several integrated SAP components:

  • SAP Build Workzone: The user interface where specialists interact with Joule
  • Joule Studio: The development environment for designing and building custom AI skills
  • SAP Build: Used to create Action Projects that expose backend functionality
  • SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP): Hosts destination configurations and orchestrates connections
  • SAP Cloud Connector: Establishes secure RFC connections between BTP and on-premise or private cloud systems
  • SAP S/4HANA / ECC: The backend ERP system containing production order data

 

Configuration Steps

Step 1: Establish RFC Connectivity

The foundation of this solution is a secure connection between your cloud and on-premise environments.

Build RFC Connection with SAP Cloud Connector:
Configure the SAP Cloud Connector to create a secure tunnel between your BTP environment and your S/4HANA or ECC system. This ensures that backend functions can be accessed securely without exposing your entire system to the internet.

 

Setup Destination in BTP Cockpit:
Create a destination in your BTP subaccount that references the RFC connection established through the Cloud Connector. This destination will be consumed by SAP Build to enable communication with your backend system.

 

Step 2: Create a BAPI/RFC Action Project

With connectivity established, the next step is to expose the specific backend functionality that Joule will use.

Create the Action Project:
Navigate to the SAP Build cockpit and create a new BAPI/RFC Action project. Search for the BAPI that retrieves production order information—for example, BAPIs related to production order management.

 

 

 

 

 

 

Test the Connection:
After creating the action project, test the connection to verify that data can be successfully retrieved from your ERP system. By default, these actions operate in READ ONLY mode to protect your business data from unintended modifications.

 

 

Optional Verification with SE37:
For deeper technical validation, you can use transaction code SE37 in your S/4HANA or ECC system to directly test the BAPI function with various parameters. This helps you understand the data structures and responses before integrating them into your Joule skill.

 

 

Step 3: Design Skills in Joule Studio

This is where you design the conversational experience and connect it to your backend data.

Skill 1: Get Production Orders

Each skill consists of three major components:

  1. Skill Description: Define the purpose of the skill and the natural language triggers that will activate it when users interact with Joule.
  2. BAPI Action: Link the skill to the BAPI/RFC Action project you created in SAP Build, establishing the connection between the conversational interface and your backend data.
  3. Send Message: Design the end-user output using the Message Configuration editor. This allows you to transform raw BAPI data into formatted, interactive cards that present information clearly and professionally.

 

 

 

Skill 2: Get Production Order Detail

Following the same pattern, create a second skill that provides detailed information about a specific production order. This allows users to drill down from a list view to detailed information through natural conversation.

 

 

 

Step 4: Deploy and Enable in Workzone

The final phase makes your custom skills accessible to end users.

Create a Shared Environment:
In Joule Studio, create a Shared environment that will serve as the runtime for your deployed skills.

 

 

Deploy Your Joule Project:
Deploy your Joule project to the Shared environment. Once deployed, verify that the project appears under Custom Capabilities.

 

 

Configure Joule in SAP Build Workzone:
Set up the integration between Joule and your SAP Build Workzone site. After completing the integration configuration, enable Joule within your specific Workzone site.

 

 

Test Your Custom Capabilities:
Access your Workzone site, open the Joule client, and test your new custom AI capabilities by asking questions in natural language about production orders.

 

 

Benefits and Outcomes

By implementing custom Joule skills, organizations can achieve:

  • Increased Productivity: Eliminate time-consuming navigation through complex ERP interfaces
  • Improved Customer Service: Enable faster, more accurate responses to customer inquiries
  • Enhanced User Experience: Provide intuitive, conversational access to critical business data
  • Secure Data Access: Maintain enterprise-grade security while democratizing data access
  • Reduced Training Requirements: Lower the learning curve for accessing ERP information

Conclusion

Building custom Joule skills via RFC represents a powerful approach to modernizing how users interact with SAP systems. By combining the conversational AI capabilities of Joule with the deep business logic in S/4HANA or ECC, organizations can create intelligent, context-aware experiences that transform daily operations.

The technical implementation, while requiring careful configuration across multiple components, provides a robust and secure foundation for extending SAP’s AI capabilities to meet specific business needs. As organizations continue their digital transformation journeys, solutions like these demonstrate how AI can be practically applied to solve real business challenges.

Additional Resources

  • Build Your First Joule Skill in Joule Studio
  • Joule Studio CodeJam
  • Build custom Joule skills for SAP and non-SAP systems using Joule Studio

entry.title: Re: 🚀 Introducing SCIM API for SAP Sales & Service Cloud v2: Seamless User Sync between Source
entry.content:
Hi,

could you explain a bit how those SCIM services are meant to be used?
When trying to call i.e. the users service with a PATCH method, the system returns the error "405 - Method not allowed". Only the GET Method is working. What needs to be done in order to use this?

Further: in the initial post it is stated, that manual user maintenance is no longer possible once this is enabled. However now that the services seem to be active in every tenant (as far as I understood), this information is no longer true?

Thanks,
Richard

entry.title: Re: How an I traverse thru tables and fill a result table?
entry.content:
Hi,

Copilot is your friend here. I got it to come up with the following example code that hopefully does what you need it to do:

CREATE PROCEDURE MY_SCHEMA::BuildDynamicResult
(
    IN  start_value  INT,
    IN  end_value    INT,
    OUT result       MyResultType
)
LANGUAGE SQLSCRIPT
AS
BEGIN

    DECLARE lv_sql   NVARCHAR(5000);
    DECLARE lv_id    INT;

    -- Initialize the output table
    result = SELECT * FROM :result LIMIT 0;

    lv_id = :start_value;

    WHILE :lv_id <= :end_value DO
        
        -- Build a dynamic SQL statement
        lv_sql = '
            SELECT ' || :lv_id || ' AS ID,
                   ''Value for ' || :lv_id || ''' AS VALUE
            FROM DUMMY
        ';

        -- Temporary container for dynamic result
        DECLARE lt_dyn TABLE LIKE result;

        lt_dyn = APPLY_FILTER(:result, '1 = 0');  -- or: lt_dyn = SELECT * FROM :result LIMIT 0;

        -- Execute dynamic SQL into lt_dyn
        EXECUTE IMMEDIATE :lv_sql INTO lt_dyn;

        -- Append to output table
        result = 
            SELECT * FROM :result
            UNION ALL
            SELECT * FROM :lt_dyn;

        -- Next iteration
        lv_id = :lv_id + 1;

    END WHILE;

END;

Regards,

Johan

entry.title: Re: Improvement Request: Hide Inactive Templates in 360 E...
entry.content:
Hi Alinachivari,

Are you referring to the issue where all 360 form templates appear when a user accesses the Executive Review? If so, there is a filter option in the top-left corner to 'Show only active templates.' If this isn't what you're looking for, could you please share a screenshot so I can better understand the requirement.

 

Thanking you.

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA migration cockpit - Testsystem & Demo Scripts
entry.content:
If you are looking for an SAP S/4HANA system for non-productive use cases such as showing demos, education yourself, or do some testing you can check the SAP S/4HANA Fully-Activated Appliance. 
With the fully-activated appliance there are also exercises and demo-scripts available for the SAP S/4HANA migration cockpit.

The latest Demo Guides for SAP S/4HANA 2025 can be found here.

Archive: Available test scripts for older versions

ApproachDemo GuideAvailable for Release
Migrate Data Using Staging TablesMigrating Bank Master Data2023 FPS02 & FPS01 / 2023 & 2022 / 2021 (FPS 01 & SP00)
 Migrating Product / Material Master Data2023 FPS03 & FPS02 & FPS01 / 2023 & 2022 / 2021 FPS02 / 2021 (FPS01 & SP00)
 Migrating G/L Accounts2023 FPS03 & FPS02 & FPS01 / 2023 & 2022 / 2021 (all FPS)
 Migration Object Modeler Cost Center Texts2023 FPS02 & FPS01 / 2023 & 2022 
Migrate Data Directly From SAP SystemActivity types / cost centers2022 
 Vendors / Suppliers2022 
 Products / Materials2022 
 Migration Object Modeler Cost Center Texts:2022 

 

How to access the system

1.  General information on the SAP S/4HANA Fully-Activated Appliance 

You can find general information on the fully-activated appliance and how to create a system on the following blog-post: https://blogs.sap.com/2018/12/12/sap-s4hana-fully-activated-appliance-create-your-sap-s4hana-1809-sy...

If you are already experienced with the underlying infrastructure of the SAP Cloud Appliance Library (SAP CAL), here is the direct links to the appliance in SAP CAL external for customers & partners.

2.  Check the Demo Guide Blog to access all the Demo Scripts for the SAP S/4HANA Fully-Activated Appliance 

Best regards,

Kirstin

entry.title: Import History from EC not include employee
entry.content:
Hi experts,

I am launching the 2025 Variable Pay worksheet.
The eligibility rule for the history import from EC requires employees to be active or on secondment as of the launch date.
I have a specific edge case: an employee who was internal until Jan 31st, 2026, and became a contingent worker starting Feb 1st, 2026. The UDF correctly reflects this: the old UserID is inactive as of Jan 31st, while the new UserID (contingent worker) is active from Feb 1st. Note that the username remained the same for both records.
Despite both UserIDs being flagged as eligible from Jan 1st, 2025, the employee is not pulled during the history import. Furthermore, the eligibility test fails to recognize the employee entirely. Could you please assist in troubleshooting why this record is being excluded?

Thanks

entry.title: Re: How to allocate fixed % (e.g. 4%) via SKF in cumulati...
entry.content:
The recommended Best Practice is

  1. If monthly flexibility is critical, use SKF with sender included as receiver, but implement strong governance. Automate SKF uploads via template. Validate that sender is always included in receiver list. Document the logic clearly for auditors and controllers.
  2. If simplicity and transparency are more important, use fixed Sender Share % and accept monthly cycle maintenance.
  3. For cumulative cycles, avoid mid-year changes in SKF ratios; instead, adjust only prospectively.

entry.title: Re: Joule Agent Test fails with an error after adding the MCP server
entry.content: I think SAP has put a fix for this issue. It started working for me today. You can also try it out.

entry.title: Re: SAP Build Apps Deprecation and The Path Forward
entry.content: This feels like a premature deprecation of SAP Build Apps. Many teams invested time and effort based on SAP’s earlier positioning of this as a key low-code pillar. While consolidation makes sense strategically, the lack of clear migration paths and timelines is concerning. It would help to see more concrete guidance and tooling support for existing customers.

entry.title: Re: Week 2 : Use Joule Chat to create ABAP RAP Application and explore the Explain feature
entry.content: Week 2 completed - Package ZAC_849949

entry.title: Re: cost element move to other existing ccs OKTZ does not...
entry.content: Changing GL assignment in OKTZ alone does not move costs from CC A to CC B. You must re‑run actual costing (CKMLCP) so the system rebuilds the cost component split with the new mapping. This is the standard way to “trigger/reset” the GL account move in actual costing.

entry.title: Incorrect GI Date in schedule line1 post plant substitution
entry.content:
For a few sales orders, when plant is getting changed during re-atp at va02 or sometimes after BOP, the GI date in schedule line 1 is not calculated as per the new route. 

But for schedule line 2 it is getting calculated correctly. This is causing sometimes the MAD in schedule line 1 to be in future as compared to schedule line 2.

Is this standard SAP behavior?

entry.title: Re: cost element move to other existing ccs OKTZ does not...
entry.content: hi Lakshmipathi, thanks for looking into this. yes that would be my expectation. But the move happened few months ago, and system is still posting to CC A.

entry.title: Re: Posting on Header Level WBS (SAP PS Public Cloud)
entry.content:
In SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud, the behavior you are seeing is standard. Budgeting in Project System (PS) is always controlled at the overall project (header WBS) level. Even if you want to supplement, transfer, or return budget at a lower‑level WBS, the system requires that the header WBS has a budget assigned first. This is because the header WBS acts as the controlling element for the project’s budget profile. Without a budget at the header, the system cannot validate or distribute budget downwards.

Why posting happened without Header Budget is because, When you post costs (actuals) to a WBS, the system allows it because cost postings are independent of budget availability. However, when you perform budget transactions (supplement, transfer, return), the system checks the budget profile rules. If the header WBS has no budget, the system raises the error: “Please assign or supplement the budget to the Header WBS.”

Hence, to resolve your error, you must first assign or supplement budget (it could be a minimal budget amount) at the header WBS level. Only then can you perform budget transfers or returns at lower WBS elements.

entry.title: Re: SAP Crystal Reports libcurl.dll Vulnerabilities
entry.content: No, just ignore the errors. Or you can try but I believe Windows may repalce them and of course not supported if you do

entry.title: which program to run to update KSSK table

entry.title: Re: Service with Advanced Execution: Process Flow 5 – Late Billing Decision in Maintenance Order
entry.content:
Hi Sraddha,

Thanks for the blog. However, when a billable maintenance order for example a PMSV order is created in IW31 and then in IW32, I do not see any billable checkbox available in the order header even though the order is in created status. However, when I am creating from PMSV order from Service order MSO it is showing the billable checkbox auto-updated with sales data. All config checked. What might be missing. Any pointer will be helpful. Version S4 2023OP.

Regards,

entry.title: Re: While export programmatically export with dot net 4.....
entry.content:
Go here and check the Platforms list to see that 4.6 is no longer supported, 4.7 or 4.8 only

Also what SP are you using, get the latest SP 39 from the link above.

It this a WEB or Desktop app?

entry.title: Re: Week 3 : Use RAP Business Logic Prediction to Implement Validation
entry.content: Week 3 challenge done - Package:ZAC_2284677

entry.title: Re: Delay one operation of task in maintenance plan
entry.content:
In standard SAP PM/maintenance planning, the scheduling logic is tied to the maintenance plan, not to individual operations inside the task list. That is why when you launch multiple plans referencing the same task list, all operations including the one with the 6‑week cycle, are scheduled together.

Having said that, you cannot delay a single operation independently inside a maintenance plan, so the cleanest solution is to use a strategy plan with multiple packages, which allows weekly operations and the 6‑week operation to be scheduled correctly without manual offsets.

entry.title: User-Export in SAC and Datasphere
entry.content:
Dear Community!

Is it possible for SAC and Datasphere users to export an overview to which teams the users are assigned to? So far I have only seen the export function for the user–role assignment. From the exported overview, it is not clear which teams these users are assigned to. 

Thanks and best wishes!

entry.title: Re: Procurement of Services in SAP Ariba
entry.content:
Hi @berrylin,

I see, you want to directly integrate SES from SAP Business Network. 

Below are the answers to the questions you have raised - 

  • Is the ExportServiceSheet web service intended for data from Ariba Buying and Invoicing solutions into SAP ERP systems? - Yes, the webservice is intended to send the SES from SAP Ariba Buying & Invoicing to ERP/S4HANA.
  • Is the ExportServiceSheet web service limited to Ariba Buying and Invoicing only? Yes, it is limited to SAP Ariba Buying & Invoicing which is why when supplier submits SES from SAP Business Network the webservice will not trigger.

entry.title: Re: How to Configure Remote ATC Between SAP BTP ABAP Envi...
entry.content:
Hello OmPrakashJha

you are trying to set up communication acenario SAP_COM_0464 for the Custom Code Migration Integration, right?

Then please check the documentation here: SAP_COM_0464

The Cloud Connector settings for RFC depend on your system landscape, you could check the documentation here: Configure the Cloud Connector
For the configuration in Cloud Connector you can use 2861842 - Custom Code Migration in SAP BTP ABAP environment: Set up SAP Cloud Connector.

You need to create an outbound communication user in you communication system for it to be selectable in the communication arrangement. This should be an ABAP user in your on-premise system to call the RFC.

The Object Provider is explained in the scenario documentation.

Please check the SAP Note 2436688 - Recommended SAP Notes for Using S/4HANA Custom Code Checks in ATC or Custom Code Migration App for the recommended SAP Notes for this scenario.

Hope this helps.

Best regards,
Peter

entry.title: Re: Where is displayed PRODCOM field?
entry.content: Did you check transaction ve47?

entry.title: Introducing the Success Guide for SAP Business Suite: Enabling Transformation and Project Success
entry.content:
We are excited to announce the release of the Success Guide – Project Experience Sharing for SAP Business Suite, aligned with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2602.

This release marks a significant milestone in the evolution of the Success Guide. What began as focused guidance for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition has now expanded into a Business Suite – oriented framework, offering broader, deeper, and more integrated insights to support complex enterprise transformation journeys.

A Strategic Shift Toward Business Suite Integration

As customers increasingly adopt a suite-first strategy, implementation success depends on more than mastering a single product. It requires a holistic understanding of how solutions interact across the enterprise landscape.

The Success Guide reflects this shift by extending beyond SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition to include cross-solution integration guidance across key SAP Business Suite components, such as:

  • SAP Cloud ERP
  • SAP Concur
  • SAP Ariba and SAP Business Network
  • ....

By consolidating insights across these solutions, the guide enables project teams to move from product-level expertise to end-to-end solution thinking, ensuring more cohesive implementations and reduced integration risks.

Built on Real Project Experience and Cross-Team Expertise

The Success Guide is not just theoretical documentation - it is grounded in real-world project experience and built through extensive collaboration across multiple SAP teams. By combining field learnings, delivery experience, and product insights, the guide captures recurring risk patterns, proven mitigation strategies, and actionable recommendations that can be directly applied in implementation projects.

This collective intelligence transforms the guide into a practical decision-support tool for project teams, rather than a static reference document.

Designed to Mitigate Risk and Accelerate Delivery

A core objective of the Success Guide is to proactively address common implementation challenges.

It provides:

  • Identified project risk points and root causes
  • Recommended mitigation actions and best practices
  • Contextualized insights aligned to SAP Activate phases
  • Integration-specific considerations across Business Suite solutions

By leveraging these insights early - especially during the Prepare and Explore phases - teams can significantly reduce rework, avoid late-stage surprises, and maintain project momentum.

Continuous Innovation Through Structured Updates

To remain relevant in a fast-evolving cloud landscape, the Success Guide follows a biannual update cycle, aligned with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition major releases.

Each update incorporates:

  • Lessons learned from recent implementation projects
  • Enhancements to existing guidance based on product evolution
  • Expanded coverage across new scenarios and integrations

Additionally, the document benefits from AI-assisted content validation, improving consistency, accuracy, and scalability of the knowledge base.

This ensures the guide remains current, actionable, and aligned with real customer needs.

More Than Documentation: A Trusted Companion for Project Success

The Success Guide is designed to be more than a knowledge repository. It acts as a trusted companion throughout the project lifecycle, helping teams to:

  • Reduce implementation risk
  • Accelerate time-to-value
  • Improve cross-team alignment
  • Leverage proven experience at scale

Whether you are a consultant, architect, or project manager, the guide provides experience-driven insights to support confident decision-making at every stage.

Access the Success Guide

The latest version of the Success Guide is available via the SAP Digital Library – Success Guide, providing centralized and curated access to all updated content.

We encourage all project team members to leverage this guide early and continuously to maximize project outcomes and drive successful SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition as well as SAP Business Suite implementations.

entry.title: Re: Transport import failing with activation errors in SAP
entry.content: Between the items "Analyzed" and "Retried", you forgot "Fixed the issues". Nobody can help you if you don't provide both the log and the detailed analysis.

entry.title: Breaking Meows: SAP Translation Hub Unveils Groundbreaking Feline Language Pack
entry.content:
After years of dedicated Animal Language Processing research (ALP), SAP is proud to launch the first-ever enterprise-grade translation service for Feline (language code: fe-LI).

Billions of cats are a key demographic in the homes of decision-makers worldwide. Their feedback on products (from couches to cat food) has, until now, been "lost in translation”. This new service bridges that critical communication gap for the global economy.

Main Highlights:

- Language variants: The model had to be trained on thousands of hours of audio to distinguish between the guttural chirps of a Maine Coon and the high-pitched, demanding yowls of a Siamese. We now have over 42 dialect-specific language packs in beta testing.

- The Ambiguity of the "Meow": Our AI team spent many months differentiating the "I’m hungry" meow from the "I’m bored" meow. They are nearly identical in frequency, but the breakthrough came when we correlated the audio with "tail-position metadata."

- Non-Verbal Integration: Vocalization is only 30% of feline communication. The biggest challenge was interpreting non-verbal cues. Our "SAP Vision AI for Felines" add-on can now interpret these signal and turn them into actionable insights.

😺Disclaimer: No cats were harmed during the testing of these features. All cats who supported were rewarded with pawsome treats. 😺

Learn More

  • SAP Translation Hub Documentation
  • LLM-based Translation Supported Languages
  • Contact us: translationhub@sap.com

Have you been waiting for support for a specific language? Let us know in the comments which languages would help your localization projects!

entry.title: Re: How to avoid latency in the Compensation Module
entry.content:
Hi @xavierlegarrec ,

Thank you, this is very useful!

I have the following case: EP only instance, VP template is quite simple, no complex formula or excessive number of fields. When the number of employees displayed on EXR  is 20-25 (all scope is 150 employees on that template), still there is a latency identified. For compensation template, there is no latency.

The only speficities I can find are that the template has the ABR settings activated and has 3 goal plans (bizgoal) configured. Is one of these can be additional reason that can explain the latency in the executive review display for VP template ?

Thank you

entry.title: Valuation of produced material - moving average price
entry.content:
Hello, 
we have the following situation: we are purchasing a trading good which is valuated with moving average price. Sometimes small adaption has to be done. Therefore a bill of material with routing is set up. Speciality: the material number does not change, the material itself is consumed in the production order, but also output of the production order. When we create a production order, the planned price does not include the routing from the bill of material - the system just valuates the ouput of the current moving average price of the input material. Only when the production order is closed the variances also settle to inventory. How can we customize the system, that in case a product with moving average valuation is produced, output of the production order should be valuated with the total planned costs and not with the actual moving averape price?

Best regards
Anja

entry.title: Pending Transport Requests from ECC to S/4HANA – Which Tool to Use?
entry.content:
Hello, community!

My company hired a consulting firm to carry out the data migration from ECC to S/4HANA, and the process was successfully completed. However, there are still some transport requests that need to be moved from ECC to S/4, and the consulting team has already finished their engagement.

Could anyone advise which tool should be used for this transport? Also, is this activity usually handled by the functional/technical team, or is it the responsibility of the Basis team?

Thanks

entry.title: Re: Transitioning into SAP Cloud (E_S4CPE path). Open to...
entry.content:
Hi @sdcasper2026-_ 

Thanks so much for the engagement!
I really appreciate it—and what a great idea you’re sharing here. It’s an honor to connect with people who are diving into the SAP ecosystem with so much focus and curiosity.

Your transition into Cloud ERP and S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition sounds exciting!
The Implementing SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition learning journey provides the material to learn how to implement SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition and get certified for  SAP CERTIFICATION • E_S4CPE SAP Certified - Implementation Consultant - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition 
It’s great to see your commitment.

Looking forward to see other to connect, exchange experiences, and the conversations!

Please let me know if you need any additional support.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Re: cost element move to other existing ccs OKTZ does not...

entry.title: Re: Import History from EC not include employee

entry.title: Re: cost element move to other existing ccs OKTZ does not...

entry.title: Re: Freight unit stage remains blocked for planning with...
entry.content: we are facing the same issue in our system. Can you please provide more information about the PPOCE setup.

entry.title: Re: How to Post Tax Amount to Expense G/L When Base Price...
entry.content:
Dear Sasitharan,

Statistical Tax + Separate Expense Condition Recommended

Make MWST statistical

In V/06 (Condition Type MWST): Set Statistical.

This prevents tax from flowing to customer account.

Create a new condition type (e.g. ZTAX_EXP), Copy MWST → ZTAX_EXP

Configure:

  • Condition class: D (Taxes)
  • Calculation type: %
  • Same access sequence as MWST
  • Account Key = ZEX (custom)
  • Not statistical

Pricing Procedure (V/08)

  • Keep MWST (statistical)
  • Add ZTAX_EXP after discount

So:

  • MWST → only for display/reporting
  • ZTAX_EXP → actual posting

Account Determination (VKOA)

Assign:

  • Account Key ZEX → Expense G/L

Now posting will be:

Account Dr/Cr

Expense G/L

Dr 5

Output Tax G/L

Cr 5

Customer

0

Regards

Soumya Nath.

How would I get by this?

entry.title: Error Importing Performance Objectives - When inserting: Objective Start, Objective Due, and Target
entry.content:
Hello Community, 

I am trying to import Performance Objective with Metric Lookup table through Import performance objectives page, however when inserting the data in the fields in the csv file: Objective_Start, Objective_Due, and Objective_Target. Note that the dates used are the Objective Plan's Start and End dates. I get this mail with the error message: 

"Row Number:1

Successfully Processed.

Row Number:2

Successfully Processed.

Row Number:3

Successfully Processed.

Row Number:4

Successfully Processed.

Row Number:5

Successfully Processed.

Row Number:6

              Processing Error:

Parsing Validations:        1

              Field Name:       Target                  Parsing Error

Row Number:7

              Processing Error:

Record does not exist:    1

                             Invalid users

Row Number:8

              Processing Error:

Record does not exist:    1

                             Invalid users

Summary:

GM Import Total Item:   0"

Additionally, when the Objective is being inserted without these data, the Objective is created as expected. 

Any thoughts? 

Thank you in advance.

Sofia

SAP SuccessFactors Performance & Goals

entry.title: SAP Build Process Automation - Notification step fails with "this.certificates is null"
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I hope you're doing well. I'm reaching out to the community because I've been stuck on this issue for a while and would really appreciate your help.

I'm currently setting up Notifications in SAP Build Process Automation following the official documentation.

 

However, every time a notification is triggered during process execution, it fails with the following error:

Error: Notification 'xxxxxxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxxxxxxxxxx' failed with the following error: 'Cannot invoke "java.util.List.stream()" because "this.certificates" is null'. 

I have created the Alert_Notification_Connectivity_ANS destination using the credentials from Work Zone Settings → Notifications (Host, Client ID, Client Secret, Token Endpoint) based on Enable Notifications 

  1. Verified the destination connection — it returns 200 OK successfully.

Is anyone else experiencing this issue, or am I missing some configuration?

Thank you so much in advance for any guidance or suggestions. I truly appreciate your time and support!

Best regards

Giang

entry.title: Error Writing into UIAD is not supported when deleting catalog via /UI2/FLPCM_CONF
entry.content:
Via /UI2/FLPCM_CONF, I have created a business catalog ZZZ, without doing anything else (i.e. not assigning any tile, etc.)

Via /UI2/FLPAM, I have created a technical catalog ZZZ with checkbox "Empty catalog".

Via /UI2/FLPCM_CONF, deleting the business catalog ZZZ does the below error:

  • Writing into UIAD is not supported (/UI2/FDM_UIAD999)

This error message also happens for other actions on the business catalog, like assigning a tile/target mapping to the catalog.

Thanks

Sandra

System: S/4HANA 2023 FPS01 ON-PREMISE, ABAP 7.58 SP 1.

entry.title: Re: Error Writing into UIAD is not supported when deleting catalog via /UI2/FLPCM_CONF
entry.content:
A technical catalog created in /UI2/FLPAM should not have the same name as a business catalog created via /UI2/FLPCM_CONF.

Solution in the current situation: delete the technical catalog ZZZ via /UI2/FLPAM, then you can delete the business catalog ZZZ via /UI2/FLPCM_CONF.

To avoid this kind of problem, add the letters "BC" in the names of the business catalogs (/UI2/FLPCM_CUST or /UI2/FLPCM_CONF) and "TC" in the names of the technical catalogs (/UI2/FLPAM).

NB: the error Writing into UIAD is not supported (/UI2/FDM_UIAD999) corresponds to the exception class /UI2/CX_FDM_OP_NOT_SUPPORTED instantiated with TEXTID = /UI2/CX_FDM_OP_NOT_SUPPORTED=>UIAD_WRITE_NOT_SUPPORTED.

entry.title: Re: How to Configure Remote ATC Between SAP BTP ABAP Envi...
entry.content: Thanks @peterpersiel for your response. However, I must admit that I’m feeling quite stuck custom code analysis catalog inside Analyze custom code analysis tile , as I can’t find any proper end‑to‑end documentation. The SAP Help Portal only provides fragmented information, and none of the pages explain the full process in a clear, sequential way. Could you please share any link or document where all the steps are described in order? Main blocker is when I go inside the catalog Custom Code Analysis -> Custom code analysis tile -> Create custom code analysis project "Object provider to remote system" is blank.How to get it.

entry.title: Re: Issues with CDS based Delta Extraction in SAP Datasphere
entry.content: The message says you want a string length 8 and a string length 10 is delivered. So check your data

entry.title: Can MRP prioritize one BOM alternative component and fall back to another based on availability?
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I have a question regarding SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition and the behavior of MRP with alternative components in production.

Let’s say I have:

  • Product A as the finished product

  • Raw Material B as the main/preferred component

  • Raw Material C as an alternative component that can also be used to produce Product A

My business requirement is the following:

When MRP runs for Product A, I want the system to first consider Raw Material B as the priority component. If B is not available in stock, then I want the system to recommend using Raw Material C instead.

So the expected logic would be something like:

  • Use B first

  • If B is unavailable, then propose C as the alternative

My understanding so far is that in standard SAP Public Cloud, alternative items in the BOM can be maintained with priority, but MRP itself may not behave as a true fallback logic during planning. It seems the alternative can be handled later at the production order/component level rather than directly in the MRP recommendation.

Could you please confirm the following:

  1. In SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, can MRP itself recommend the alternative component automatically based on stock availability?

  2. Or is this only handled later by the planner in the production order?

  3. If standard MRP does not support this exact fallback behavior, what is the best-practice approach in Public Cloud?

Thank you in advance.

entry.title: Re: Activate Joule in SAP Concur - a Step-by-Step Guide
entry.content: @dyaryura - Yes, thank you. This feedback has been shared with the product team. They are working on it.

entry.title: Shape the Future of AI: Join the SAP Enterprise Service Management – Hands-on Experience
entry.content:
Are you ready to influence the next generation of SAP-managed integrations?

We are excited to announce two unique opportunities for customers and partners to get hands-on experience with
SAP Enterprise Service Management (ESM) and SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. 
We are looking for participants to collaborate with us, validate new features, and provide early feedback that will directly impact
SAP’s product roadmap.
Whether you are focused on the technical "how-to" of onboarding or the functional power of Generative AI, we have a track for you. 

Choose Your Path
We are offering two SAP Integration Experience Feedback (IEF) tracks for our customers and partners.
You can participate in one or both, depending on their expertise:

Track 1: Provisioning & Setup (The Implementation Deep-Dive)

Focus on the end-to-end journey of getting the system up and running.

  • What you’ll do: Experience the SAP-managed integration and E2E customer onboarding, including SAP for Me
    and SAP Central Business Configuration (CBC).
  • The Goal: Help us automate and reduce manual effort in the Cloud ALM experience.
  • Best for: Project Leads, Business Process Consultants, and Technical Experts.
  • For Track 1 please register here.

Track 2: Functional & embedded AI Experience (The Business User Deep-Dive)

Focus on the business process and the power of embedded AI.

  • What you’ll do: Validate the "Backend Entity Extraction" feature - a Generative AI tool that automatically identifies
    dispute amounts, currencies, and reasons from emails.
  • The Goal: Evaluate AI Quality (Reliability, Relevance, and Robustness) and test the seamless data flow between
    SAP Enterprise Service Management and SAP S/4HANA     Cloud Public Edition.
  • Best for: Business Process Experts and those familiar with Dispute Management and Master Data Synchronization.
  • For Track 2 please register here.

Why Participate?

  • First-Hand Access: Get early access to the AI-embedded Dispute Management scenario
    as part of SAP Enterprise Service Management.
  • Influence SAP: Your feedback directly improves the quality and direction of SAP-managed integrations.
  • Recognition: Receive a confirmation of participation from SAP upon completion.

Engagement Details

  • Timeline: April – October 2026
  • Commitment: Includes an initial briefing, a kick-off demo, and 2 - 3 days of hands-on activities in a provided
    SAP landscape.
  • Location: Worldwide (Remote sessions)
  • Prerequisite: A valid Feedback Agreement (FBA) with SAP is required (if not available, we can help you set this up after registration!).


Looking foward to receive your registrations!
Your SAP Integration Experience Feedback Team

entry.title: Re: SAP Analytics Cloud Connection To Sharepoint via Open...
entry.content: It is known issue and please refer to SAP KBA: 3731970 - Microsoft ACS authentication retirement may impact SharePoint connections in SAP Analytics Cloud via Open Connectors - SAP for Me

entry.title: SAC Report is unable to display on webpage but able to export all data
entry.content:
We have SAC report called GST Report, initially it was built using SAP BW as backend but since we're moving to SAP Datasphere and now since the report is pointing to datasphere the report has 1 tab which does not display report data and throws error: 

 

Server ErrorThe data cannot be displayed: the query process was stopped before it finished because it returned too many records. Please try filtering your data

entry.content:
Hello Shilpa,

I had completed this additional task. Please find the package name - ZAC_145060

Thanks,

Ojeswini

entry.title: Re: How to place comma from integer or decimal or any datatype columns of a table

entry.content: @EugenioPresno That's very a good 1st of April joke

entry.title: Re: SAP Analytics Cloud Connection To Sharepoint via Open...
entry.content: Thanks - yes - SAP sent this to me this morning as a response to my SAP call. We just need a fix in SAC now....

entry.title: How to Configure Remote ATC Between SAP BTP ABAP Environment and On‑Premise S/4HANA (Step‑by‑Step)?
entry.content:
* Main issue is that I am not able to  find " Object provider for remote system" under custom code analysis tile.

Cloud Connector RFC Mapping – Not Sure Which Settings to Use

When adding an on‑premise ABAP system in Cloud Connector (Protocol = RFC), I am asked to choose:

  • With load balancing (SID + message server)
  • Without load balancing (app server + instance number)

I am unsure which one is correct for Remote ATC, and what the exact host/port values should be.

Communication System in BTP ABAP Does Not Show RFC Option

After setting up the Cloud Connector entries, when I create a Communication System in the BTP ABAP Environment, the RFC outbound service is missing, and I cannot create the RFC destination required for Remote ATC.

Object Providers Do Not Appear in Custom Code Migration App

In the Custom Code Analysis Project setup screen,
“Object Provider to Remote System” shows No data available, meaning I cannot continue the Remote ATC configuration.

What Is the Correct End‑to‑End Setup?

I am following SAP’s Remote ATC documentation (Cloud Connector + Communication System + Object Provider), but there seems to be missing prerequisites or configuration steps.

Can someone guide me with the correct end‑to‑end steps?

Specifically:

  • Which RFC configuration should be used in Cloud Connector (load balancing or not)?
  • What are the correct message server / app server details for S/4HANA?
  • How to ensure the Communication System in BTP ABAP recognizes the RFC connection?
  • How to make Object Providers appear under ATC Administration → Setup?

Any screenshots, step-by-step instructions, or clarifications from someone who has successfully configured Remote ATC would be greatly appreciated.

I tried to follow few SAP blogs but they are very old to follow up.

Thank you!

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

I must admit that I’m feeling quite stuck custom code analysis catalog inside Analyze custom code analysis tile , as I can’t find any proper end‑to‑end documentation. The SAP Help Portal only provides fragmented information, and none of the pages explain the full process in a clear, sequential way. Could you please share any link or document where all the steps are described in order? Main blocker is when I go inside the catalog Custom Code Analysis -> Custom code analysis tile -> Create custom code analysis project "Object provider to remote system" is blank.How to get it.

entry.title: From insight to impact: The future of planning 2.0 with SAP agent driven experiences
entry.content:
Planning.  But faster.  Smarter.  Way more flexible.

We’re bringing you a first look at what’s next: agent-driven planning in SAP Analytics Cloud.

Here’s the deal, intelligent agents that accelerate forecasting, a simulation layer that lets you iterate freely (finally), and clear partner opportunities across advisory, extensions, and packaged solutions.

No fluff.  Just what’s changing, why it matters, and how you can turn it into value, fast.

Plus, a preview of what’s coming ahead of SAP Sapphire.

If you’re in EPM, xP&A, or anything planning-related… you’ll want in.

Register now.  https://partneredge.sap.com/en/library/education/psd/2026/mar/e_oe_te_w_PSD_WEB_00013550.html

#SAP #SAPBTP #SAPPartners #SAPEcosystem #EPM #xPA #SAPInnovation #CloudPlanning

entry.title: Dark Mode Beta Phase Complete: See What's New
entry.content:
We are excited to announce that Dark Mode is now live as the Beta Phase is complete! 

Last year, after listening to your feedback, we launched Dark Mode in beta, giving you the chance to choose your preferred visual experience. 

Since then, you have shared suggestions on how to make this feature more polished and seamless. With that in mind, we are happy to introduce the following improvements: 

  1. Light/Dark Mode Toggle 

A new toggle replaces the temporary text-based switch, making it easier to switch between themes for a more personalized browsing experience.

 

  1. Dark Mode Improvements for DSA Reporting Form 

The "Report Inappropriate Content" form has been updated to ensure all table content is visible and readable in dark mode. Previously unreadable white-on-white elements now follow the correct dark theme styling. 

Why this matters?  

Dark Mode improves how you experience the SAP Community everyday:  

  • More comfortable browsing: Reduced eye strain, especially in low-light environments, makes it easier to stay engaged for longer.   
  • Greater control over your experience: The new toggle lets you instantly switch between light and dark modes, so you can choose what works best for you at any moment.   
  • Better readability where it counts: Improvements to key areas like the reporting form to ensure content is always clear and accessible, no matter the theme.

entry.title: SAP Convergent Mediation by DigitalRoute – New Release Version 9.5
entry.content:
SAP Convergent Mediation by DigitalRoute – New Release Version 9.5

New Release

Public

March 2026

We are excited to announce the release and general availability of SAP Convergent Mediation 9.5!

With this release, we continue our focus on three key value areas: productivity and efficiency, quality and reliability, and security and compliance. It introduces enhancements that simplify configuration management and deployment across environments, extend persistence options for real-time workflows, and strengthen control of workflow execution.

These capabilities enable more consistent automation in CI/CD pipelines, ensure predictable and transparent operations, and support secure, compliant handling of data and configurations across all environments. 

Some of the key features and enhancements are:

SQL for Aggregation Storage in Real-time Workflows

This enhancement adds SQL as a storage option for aggregation in real-time workflows. Previously, SQL storage only existed for batch workflows.

With SQL storage, you can persist session data in real-time workflows without relying on disk, Couchbase or Redis. The feature reuses and extends functionality already developed for batch aggregation, adapting it for real-time workflow scenarios.

For SAP ECS customers this adds support for managing and storing real-time workflow run-time aggregation data on HANA database instead of filesystem. Thus allowing disaster recovery replication process to be more frequent as it's based on HANA database instead of filesystem replication.

Direct Creation of Database Tables

This enhancement enables you to create the required database tables for Aggregation, Duplicate UDR, Inter Workflow, and Data Veracity directly from the Desktop, and not have to ask your DBA to create the tables for you. Further lowering the threshold for customers that want to store all their run-time data on HANA database instead of filesystem, for more frequent replication of run-time data to disaster recovery site.

Previously, creating or modifying tables in some environments required the generated SQL code to be run separately. This could mean submitting it through service tickets for execution, causing delays and extending project lead times. With this feature, you can initiate and complete table creation requests directly in the profile configuration, reducing dependency on external tools and support processes. This supports productivity, faster project delivery, and improved usability.

REST Interface for Execution Contexts

This enhancement lets you monitor and deactivate standalone workflows even when the platform server is offline—crucial during upgrades when workflows continue running independently. The extended Operations REST interface provides methods to query workflow details and deactivate them, supporting automation and integration with operational tools. Existing properties still control interface availability.

By removing platform dependency for workflow management and enabling direct, automated control, this feature ensures reliable operations during upgrades or planned downtime.

SOC 1/SOX Guidance

If you are subject to SOC 1/SOX compliance, you may have been asked: 

How does SAP Convergent Mediation help me demonstrate SOC 1 control operation? 

In this release, we are publishing a set of pages that map MediationZone capabilities to SOC 1/SOX control objectives, so you can show how the product supports your financial reporting controls. 

For the System Properties, Desktop Properties, and Legacy Desktop properties, we indicate: 

  1. Whether it is in scope for SOC 1.
  2. The primary categories supported are Confidentiality, Integrity, or Availability.
  3. The Reason, a short explanation of the control value, for example, “enforces encryption,” “prevents resource exhaustion,” or “standardizes log timestamps for audit”. This helps you baseline configurations.

Around the mapping, we have added information to help you put these controls in operation:

  • Change Management explains how to route MediationZone configuration changes through approvals and capture evidence
  • Sensitive Access Monitoring outlines approaches for governing administrator access, permissions, and events
  • User Access Monitoring covers provisioning, lifecycle, and the kinds of artifacts auditors typically request.

Each section clarifies the split of responsibilities: what SAP Convergent Mediation provides, what you configure, and what must be proven by your own processes. If you are preparing for a SOX cycle, start at the SOC 1 and SOX Guidance page. Use the properties mapping to set or verify your configuration baseline, then pull the Change/Sensitive Access/User Access pages into your control narratives and evidence plans.

We hope this guidance saves your team time and gives your auditors the confidence they need. 

We encourage you to explore all the detailed features and enhancements below to gain a comprehensive understanding of the capabilities in MediationZone 9.5:

  • SOC 1 / SOX Compliance Guidance

New Features

Usability

  • Direct Creation of Database Tables for Data Veracity, Aggregation, Inter Workflow and Duplicate UDR
  • Topic Creation for Kafka Batch Transaction Handler

Additional Functionality Made Available in Desktop

  • Adding Comments to Workflows and Agents
  • ECS Inspector Bulk Edit and Restricted Fields

Integration and Compliance

  • SQL Aggregation Storage for Real-time Workflows
  • SOC 1 / SOX Compliance Guidance

Functionality Improvements

  • REST Interface in EC
  • SOAP 1.2 Support for Web Service
  • External Reference Support for Diameter Routing Profile
  • Load Balancer Added to the TCP/IP Processing Agent

Usability Improvements

  • Inline Editing Added to Workflow Table

Documentation Improvements

  • New Kafka documentation improvements
  • Clarification of Terminology for WorkflowKey 

More information 

  • SAP Product Page and Product Video 
  • SAP Convergent Mediation 9.5 Release (DigitalRoute InfoZone)
  • SAP Convergent Mediation 9.5 Release Notes (DigitalRoute InfoZone)
  • SAP Convergent Mediation 9.5 Documentation (DigitalRoute InfoZone)
  • SAP Convergent Mediation 9.5 Composite SAP Note and SAP CM Help Portal page
  • SAP Convergent Mediation 4.x End of Mainstream Maintenance (describing the need for customer still on SAP Convergent Mediation 4.x to upgrade to latest SAP Convergent Mediation release)

entry.title: Apply date granularity in SAC story using a filter or input control
entry.content:
Hi experts!!

Is there a way to apply a input control or filter to define date granularity as month, year or quarter, month, year?

entry.title: Re: Which Idoc or RFC/ BADI / EXIT triggered to EWM while doing partial usage decision in S4

entry.title: Re: Batch management at 'Plant level and 'Material level' in Master data but 'at material level' in
entry.content: Hope only difference is at the table level. If MARC-XCHPF is checked then MCHA table gets updated- If the MARA-XCHPF is checked then MCH1 table is getting updated.

entry.title: SAP Live Sessions coming up in April
entry.content:
Boost your SAP skills with these live sessions* coming up in April:

Implementing a Data Lake in SAP HANA Cloud7th April 2pm CEST
Introduction to SAP Business Data Cloud13th April 9.30am CEST
Getting Started with the SAP HANA Cloud Knowledge Graph24th April 9.30am CET

* Subscription required to SAP Learning Hub
